ingredients.item: 1 pound (454 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes
ingredients.original: 1 pound (454 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes

ingredients.item: 12 ounces (350 g) cremini mushrooms (or any other mushroom variety), sliced
ingredients.original: 12 ounces (350 g) cremini mushrooms (or any other mushroom variety), sliced

ingredients.item: 2 large yellow potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes
ingredients.original: 2 large yellow potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes

ingredients.item: 3 cups (720 ml) chicken broth or water, divided
ingredients.original: 3 cups (720 ml) chicken broth or water, divided

ingredients.item: 1 TO MAKE THE CHICKEN: In a large bowl, mix together the 1 teaspoon curry powder, ½ teaspoon salt, pepper, and cornstarch. Add the chicken cubes and thoroughly coat with the seasoning mix.
ingredients.original: 1 TO MAKE THE CHICKEN: In a large bowl, mix together the 1 teaspoon curry powder, ½ teaspoon salt, pepper, and cornstarch. Add the chicken cubes and thoroughly coat with the seasoning mix.

ingredients.item: 2 In a large skillet at least 2 inches (5 cm) deep, heat the 2 tablespoons vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chicken cubes, working in batches if required, and sear for 1 to 2 minutes per side, until browned. Transfer to a plate.
ingredients.original: 2 In a large skillet at least 2 inches (5 cm) deep, heat the 2 tablespoons vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chicken cubes, working in batches if required, and sear for 1 to 2 minutes per side, until browned. Transfer to a plate.

ingredients.item: 3 TO MAKE THE CURRY: In the same skillet the chicken was cooked in, heat the 1 tablespoon vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the onion and ½ teaspoon of the salt and cook, stirring often, for 3 to 4 minutes, until the onion is softened. Add the mushrooms and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, for another 5 to 7 minutes, until browned.
ingredients.original: 3 TO MAKE THE CURRY: In the same skillet the chicken was cooked in, heat the 1 tablespoon vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the onion and ½ teaspoon of the salt and cook, stirring often, for 3 to 4 minutes, until the onion is softened. Add the mushrooms and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, for another 5 to 7 minutes, until browned.

ingredients.item: 4 Add the potatoes, 2½ teaspoons curry powder, pepper, and remaining 1 teaspoon salt. Mix well and cook for another 3 to 4 minutes. Add the peas and 2 cups (480 ml) of the broth. Bring to a simmer, then cover the skillet with a lid and let cook for 10 minutes.
ingredients.original: 4 Add the potatoes, 2½ teaspoons curry powder, pepper, and remaining 1 teaspoon salt. Mix well and cook for another 3 to 4 minutes. Add the peas and 2 cups (480 ml) of the broth. Bring to a simmer, then cover the skillet with a lid and let cook for 10 minutes.

ingredients.item: 5 Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix the cornstarch with the remaining 1 cup (240 ml) broth and whisk until a smooth slurry forms.
ingredients.original: 5 Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix the cornstarch with the remaining 1 cup (240 ml) broth and whisk until a smooth slurry forms.

ingredients.item: 6 After the 10 minutes, uncover the pan and add the slurry. Add the chicken back into the pan, mix well, and cook for another 15 to 20 minutes, uncovered, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
ingredients.original: 6 After the 10 minutes, uncover the pan and add the slurry. Add the chicken back into the pan, mix well, and cook for another 15 to 20 minutes, uncovered, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
